title: phegopteris urophylla
intro: Phegopteris urophylla, also known as the autumn fern or golden lady fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting digestion, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The herbal preparations of Phegopteris urophylla include decoctions, infusions, and powders, which can be used to make tea, capsules, or topical creams, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
